contentBlocks.original:
भवानि स्तोतुं त्वां प्रभवति चतुर्भिर्न वदनैः
प्रजानामीशानस्त्रिपुरमथनः पञ्चभिरपि ।
न षड्भिः सेनानीर्दशशतमुखैरप्यहिपतिः
तदान्येषां केषां कथय कथमस्मिन्नवसरः ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavāni stōtuṃ tvāṃ prabhavati chaturbhirna vadanaiḥ
prajānāmīśānastripuramathanaḥ pañchabhirapi ।
na ṣaḍbhiḥ sēnānīrdaśaśatamukhairapyahipatiḥ
tadānyēṣāṃ kēṣāṃ kathaya kathamasminnavasaraḥ ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Bhavānī — Brahmā, the lord of all beings, cannot praise you even with four faces; nor can the destroyer of Tripura (Śiva) with five; nor Ṣaṇmukha with six; nor Hari with a thousand. So how then can I praise you, O Tripura — you whose praises even the gods fail to exhaust?
contentBlocks.meanings: bhavāni: O Bhavānī | stotum tvām: to praise you | prabhavati: is capable | caturbhiḥ na vadanaiḥ: not with four faces | prajānām īśāna: lord of all beings (Brahmā) | tripura-mathanaḥ: destroyer of Tripura (Śiva) | pañcabhir api: even with five | na ṣaḍbhiḥ: not with six | senanīḥ: general (Skanda/Ṣaṇmukha) | prajā-īśaḥ: lord of beings (Viṣṇu, Hari) | sahasreṇa: with a thousand

contentBlocks.original:
घृतक्षीरद्राक्षामधुमधुरिमा कैरपि पदैः
विशिष्यानाख्येयो भवति रसनामात्र विषयः ।
तथा ते सौन्दर्यं परमशिवदृङ्मात्रविषयः
कथङ्कारं ब्रूमः सकलनिगमागोचरगुणे ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ghṛtakṣīradrākṣāmadhumadhurimā kairapi padaiḥ
viśiṣyānākhyēyō bhavati rasanāmātra viṣayaḥ ।
tathā tē saundaryaṃ paramaśivadṛṅmātraviṣayaḥ
kathaṅkāraṃ brūmaḥ sakalanigamāgōcharaguṇē ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as the sweetness of ghee, milk, raisins, and honey cannot be expressed in words — it remains only a matter for the tongue alone — so too your beauty, O Tripura Sundarī, cannot be described but only experienced.
contentBlocks.meanings: ghṛta-kṣīra-drākṣā-madhu-madhurima: sweetness of ghee, milk, raisins, and honey | kaiḥ api padaiḥ: by any words | viśiṣya-anākhyeyaḥ: especially inexpressible | rasanā-mātra-viṣayaḥ: only a matter for the tongue alone | tathā te saundarya: so too your beauty | tripura-sundari: O Tripura Sundarī

contentBlocks.original:
मुखे ते ताम्बूलं नयनयुगले कज्जलकला
ललाटे काश्मीरं विलसति गले मौक्तिकलता ।
स्फुरत्काञ्ची शाटी पृथुकटितटे हाटकमयी
भजामि त्वां गौरीं नगपतिकिशोरीमविरतम् ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mukhē tē tāmbūlaṃ nayanayugaḻē kajjalakalā
lalāṭē kāśmīraṃ vilasati gaḻē mauktikalatā ।
sphuratkāñchī śāṭī pṛthukaṭitaṭē hāṭakamayī
bhajāmi tvāṃ gaurīṃ nagapatikiśōrīmaviratam ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.translation: Betel-leaf adorns your face; kohl-art adorns your eyes; saffron paste adorns the forehead; a pearl-creeper adorns the neck; a gleaming kāñcī-girdle and golden-threaded garments adorn your hips; camphor and sandalwood adorn your body. May your form, O mother, remain in my heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: mukhe te tāmbūlam: betel-leaf on your face | nayana-yugale kajjala-kalā: kohl-art on the eyes | lalāṭe kāśmīram: saffron paste on the forehead | gale mauktika-latā: pearl-creeper at the neck | sphurat-kāñcī: gleaming kāñcī-girdle | śāṭī: garment | campaka-candana: camphor and sandalwood | hṛdaye: in my heart

contentBlocks.original:
विराजन्मन्दारद्रुमकुसुमहारस्तनतटी
नदद्वीणानादश्रवणविलसत्कुण्डलगुणा
नताङ्गी मातङ्गी रुचिरगतिभङ्गी भगवती
सती शम्भोरम्भोरुहचटुलचक्षुर्विजयते ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
virājanmandāradrumakusumahārastanataṭī
nadadvīṇānādaśravaṇavilasatkuṇḍalaguṇā
natāṅgī mātaṅgī ruchiragatibhaṅgī bhagavatī
satī śambhōrambhōruhachaṭulachakṣurvijayatē ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.translation: She whose breast-region shines with a garland of the Mandāra tree's blossoms, adorned with the sound of a ringing vīṇā; who is slightly bowed — the great Mātaṅgī — with graceful movement, whose eyes are as beautiful as a deer's — she who has accepted (absorbed into herself) Śiva — salutations to that goddess.
contentBlocks.meanings: virājat-mandāra-druma-kusuma-hāra-stana-taṭī: whose breast-region shines with a garland of Mandāra blossoms | nadat-vīṇā-nāda-śravaṇa-vilasat-kuṇḍala: adorned with vīṇā sound and gleaming earrings | natāṅgī: slightly bowed | mātaṅgī: the great Mātaṅgī (an aspect of the goddess) | rucira-gati-bhaṅgī: graceful movement | aṅgī-kṛta-śivā: who has accepted/absorbed Śiva

contentBlocks.original:
नवीनार्कभ्राजन्मणिकनकभूषणपरिकरैः
वृताङ्गी सारङ्गीरुचिरनयनाङ्गीकृतशिवा ।
तडित्पीता पीताम्बरललितमञ्जीरसुभगा
ममापर्णा पूर्णा निरवधिसुखैरस्तु सुमुखी ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navīnārkabhrājanmaṇikanakabhūṣaṇaparikaraiḥ
vṛtāṅgī sāraṅgīruchiranayanāṅgīkṛtaśivā ।
taḍitpītā pītāmbaralalitamañjīrasubhagā
mamāparṇā pūrṇā niravadhisukhairastu sumukhī ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adorned with gem-and-gold ornaments blazing like the newly risen sun — with a doe-eyed beautiful vision that has made Śiva her own — yellow as lightning, wearing a lustrous yellow garment, holding a parrot and a bouquet — she who illuminates the night with the light of the moon — salutations to Rājarājeśvarī.
contentBlocks.meanings: navīna-arka-bhrājat: blazing like the newly risen sun | maṇi-kanaka-bhūṣaṇa-parikara: gem-and-gold ornaments | vṛta-aṅgī: adorned body | sāraṅgī-rucira-nayana: doe-eyed beautiful vision | aṅgī-kṛta-śivā: who has made Śiva her own | taḍit-pīta: yellow as lightning | pīta-ambara: yellow garment | kīra-dhṛta: holding a parrot | rājarājeśvarī: Rājarājeśvarī (queen of queens)

contentBlocks.original:
हिमाद्रेः सम्भूता सुललितकरैः पल्लवयुता
सुपुष्पा मुक्ताभिर्भ्रमरकलिता चालकभरैः ।
कृतस्थाणुस्थाना कुचफलनता सूक्तिसरसा
रुजां हन्त्री गन्त्री विलसति चिदानन्दलतिका ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
himādrēḥ sambhūtā sulalitakaraiḥ pallavayutā
supuṣpā muktābhirbhramarakalitā chālakabharaiḥ ।
kṛtasthāṇusthānā kuchaphalanatā sūktisarasā
rujāṃ hantrī gantrī vilasati chidānandalatikā ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.translation: Born of the Himalaya — with tender creeper-like hands — adorned with beautiful flowers — with locks twined with bees and pearls — who has made Śiva her place of residence — the very embodiment of all three worlds — [I meditate upon that goddess].
contentBlocks.meanings: himādreḥ sambhūtā: born of the Himalaya | su-lalita-karaiḥ pallava-yutā: with tender-creeper hands adorned with blossoms | su-puṣpā: adorned with beautiful flowers | muktābhiḥ bhramara-kalitā: twined with pearls and bees | calaka-bharaiḥ: with locks | kṛta-sthāṇu-sthānā: who has made Śiva her place of residence | tri-loka-maya-māyā: embodying the three worlds

contentBlocks.original:
सपर्णामाकीर्णां कतिपयगुणैः सादरमिह
श्रयन्त्यन्ये वल्लीं मम तु मतिरेवं विलसति ।
अपर्णैका सेव्या जगति सकलैर्यत्परिवृतः
पुराणोऽपि स्थाणुः फलति किल कैवल्यपदवीम् ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saparṇāmākīrṇāṃ katipayaguṇaiḥ sādaramiha
śrayantyanyē vallīṃ mama tu matirēvaṃ vilasati ।
aparṇaikā sēvyā jagati sakalairyatparivṛtaḥ
purāṇō'pi sthāṇuḥ phalati kila kaivalyapadavīm ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.translation: Others seek refuge in other creepers — honored with various qualities. But my mind moves thus: only Aparṇā (Pārvatī, the leafless one) is to be served — she who is a creeper with leaves, flowers, and blossoms that never fade, yielding all fruits to those who seek refuge in her.
contentBlocks.meanings: sa-parṇām: creeper with leaves | ākīrṇām: covered/full | katipaya-guṇaiḥ: with various qualities | sādaram: honorably | śrayanti: seek refuge | anye vallīm: others in other creepers | mama tu matiḥ evam vilasati: but my mind moves thus | aparṇa: the leafless/Pārvatī (who discarded even leaves in her austerity) | ekā sevyā: alone to be served | sarva-phala-pradā: yielding all fruits

contentBlocks.original:
विधात्री धर्माणां त्वमसि सकलाम्नायजननी
त्वमर्थानां मूलं धनदनमनीयाङ्घ्रिकमले ।
त्वमादिः कामानां जननि कृतकन्दर्पविजये
सतां मुक्तेर्बीजं त्वमसि परमब्रह्ममहिषी ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidhātrī dharmāṇāṃ tvamasi sakalāmnāyajananī
tvamarthānāṃ mūlaṃ dhanadanamanīyāṅghrikamalē ।
tvamādiḥ kāmānāṃ janani kṛtakandarpavijayē
satāṃ muktērbījaṃ tvamasi paramabrahmamahiṣī ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you whose lotus-feet are beautiful as Kubera's treasures — you are the orderer of all dharmas, the mother of all sacred teachings (āmnāya). You are the root of all wealth. You are the beginning of all desires. You are the foundation of liberation. Therefore — I bow to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: vidhātrī dharmāṇām: orderer of all dharmas | sakala-āmnāya-jananī: mother of all sacred teachings | artha-mūlam: root of wealth | dhana-da-namanīya-aṅghri-kamale: O you whose lotus-feet are beautiful as Kubera's treasures | kāmānām ādiḥ: the beginning of all desires | mokṣasya ādhāram: the foundation of liberation | ataḥ: therefore

contentBlocks.original:
प्रभूता भक्तिस्ते यदपि न ममालोलमनसः
त्वया तु श्रीमत्या सदयमवलोक्योऽहमधुना ।
पयोदः पानीयं दिशति मधुरं चातकमुखे
भृशं शङ्के कैर्वा विधिभिरनुनीता मम मतिः ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prabhūtā bhaktistē yadapi na mamālōlamanasaḥ
tvayā tu śrīmatyā sadayamavalōkyō'hamadhunā ।
payōdaḥ pānīyaṃ diśati madhuraṃ chātakamukhē
bhṛśaṃ śaṅkē kairvā vidhibhiranunītā mama matiḥ ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.translation: Though my devotion to you is not great — my mind being restless — yet you, O Śrīmatī (Lakṣmī-goddess), should look upon me now with compassion. A cloud gives water — and yet a plant does not ask for it. [Similarly, your grace comes naturally without my deserving it.]
contentBlocks.meanings: prabhūtā bhaktiḥ te: though my devotion to you is great/not great | na mama: not mine | ālola-manasaḥ: of restless mind | tvayā tu: but by you | śrīmatyā: O Śrīmatī (the auspicious one, Lakṣmī-goddess) | sadayam: with compassion | avalokyaḥ aham: I am to be looked upon | payo-daḥ: a cloud | pānīyam: water | diśati: gives | na vṛkṣaḥ yācate: and a plant does not ask

contentBlocks.original:
कृपापाङ्गालोकं वितर तरसा साधुचरिते
न ते युक्तोपेक्षा मयि शरणदीक्षामुपगते ।
न चेदिष्टं दद्यादनुपदमहो कल्पलतिका
विशेषः सामान्यैः कथमितरवल्लीपरिकरैः ॥ 10॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛpāpāṅgālōkaṃ vitara tarasā sādhucharitē
na tē yuktōpēkṣā mayi śaraṇadīkṣāmupagatē ।
na chēdiṣṭaṃ dadyādanupadamahō kalpalatikā
viśēṣaḥ sāmānyaiḥ kathamitaravallīparikaraiḥ ॥ 10॥
contentBlocks.translation: O one of good conduct — quickly grant your compassionate sidelong glance! It is not right for you to neglect me who have taken the vow of taking refuge. If the wish-fulfilling tree does not give what is wished — then why do people resort to it? What is its value then?
contentBlocks.meanings: kṛpā-paṅga-ālokam: compassionate sidelong glance | vitara tarasā: grant quickly | sādhu-carite: O one of good conduct | na te yuktā upekṣā: it is not right for you to neglect | mayi: toward me | śaraṇa-dīkṣām upagata: who have taken the vow of taking refuge | na ced: if not | iṣṭam dadyāt: gives what is wished | anu-kalpam: then the wish-fulfilling tree | kim artham: what purpose/value

contentBlocks.original:
महान्तं विश्वासं तव चरणपङ्केरुहयुगे
निधायान्यन्नैवाश्रितमिह मया दैवतमुमे ।
तथापि त्वच्चेतो यदि मयि न जायेत सदयं
निरालम्बो लम्बोदरजननि कं यामि शरणम् ॥ 11॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāntaṃ viśvāsaṃ tava charaṇapaṅkēruhayugē
nidhāyānyannaivāśritamiha mayā daivatamumē ।
tathāpi tvachchētō yadi mayi na jāyēta sadayaṃ
nirālambō lambōdarajanani kaṃ yāmi śaraṇam ॥ 11॥
contentBlocks.translation: Placing great faith in your two lotus-feet, I have not taken refuge in any other deity here — yet if your heart is still indifferent to me, O Umā — then to whom, alas, should I go? Who is there for the refuge of the abandoned?
contentBlocks.meanings: mahāntam viśvāsam: great faith | tava caraṇa-paṅkerūha-yugale: in your two lotus-feet | nidhāya: having placed | anyan na eva: no other | āśritam: have taken refuge | iha: here | mayā daivatam: any deity by me | umā: O Umā (Pārvatī) | tathāpi: yet | tvac-ceto: your heart | yadi niṣkaruṇam: if indifferent/without compassion | aham: I | kva gacche: where shall I go | kaḥ: who | viśramaḥ: rest/refuge | śraṇa-āgatāṃ: for the abandoned/those who have come for refuge

contentBlocks.original:
अयः स्पर्शे लग्नं सपदि लभते हेमपदवीं
यथा रथ्यापाथः शुचि भवति गङ्गौघमिलितम् ।
तथा तत्तत्पापैरतिमलिनमन्तर्मम यदि
त्वयि प्रेम्णासक्तं कथमिव न जायेत विमलम् ॥ 12॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ayaḥ sparśē lagnaṃ sapadi labhatē hēmapadavīṃ
yathā rathyāpāthaḥ śuchi bhavati gaṅgaughamilitam ।
tathā tattatpāpairatimalinamantarmama yadi
tvayi prēmṇāsaktaṃ kathamiva na jāyēta vimalam ॥ 12॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as iron that is in contact with [the philosopher's stone] immediately attains the status of gold — just as street-water becomes pure when it joins the flood of the Gaṅgā — so too, one extremely tainted by various sins, having attained your proximity, O Bhavānī, becomes pure.
contentBlocks.meanings: ayaḥ sparśe lagnam: iron that is in contact with | sapadi: immediately | hema-padavīm: gold status | labhate: attains | yathā: just as | rathyā-pāthaḥ: street-water | śuci bhavati: becomes pure | gaṅgā-augha-militam: joining the flood of the Gaṅgā | tathā: so too | tat-tat-pāpaiḥ: by various sins | ati-kaluṣam: extremely tainted | bhavānī: O Bhavānī | tava sāmīpyāt: by your proximity | śuddha: pure

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदन्यस्मादिच्छाविषयफललाभे न नियमः
त्वमर्थानामिच्छाधिकमपि समर्था वितरणे ।
इति प्राहुः प्राञ्चः कमलभवनाद्यास्त्वयि मनः
त्वदासक्तं नक्तं दिवमुचितमीशानि कुरु तत् ॥ 13॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadanyasmādichChāviṣayaphalalābhē na niyamaḥ
tvamarthānāmichChādhikamapi samarthā vitaraṇē ।
iti prāhuḥ prāñchaḥ kamalabhavanādyāstvayi manaḥ
tvadāsaktaṃ naktaṃ divamuchitamīśāni kuru tat ॥ 13॥
contentBlocks.translation: There is no certainty that [desires] will be fulfilled from anyone other than you. You alone, O Āmnāya, are capable of granting even more than what is desired. Thus the ancient ones have spoken — and tradition itself shows this. Therefore I take refuge only in you, Bhavānī.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvad-anyasmāt: from anyone other than you | icchā-viṣaya-phala-lābhe: in obtaining the fruits of desired objects | na niyamaḥ: no certainty | tvam: you | artha-ānām: of desired objects | icchā-adhikam api: even more than what is desired | samarthā: capable | vitaraṇe: in granting | iti prāhuḥ prāñcaḥ: thus the ancient ones have spoken | āmnāya: O Āmnāya (you who are the sacred tradition itself)

contentBlocks.original:
स्फुरन्नानारत्नस्फटिकमयभित्तिप्रतिफल
त्त्वदाकारं चञ्चच्छशधरकलासौधशिखरम् ।
मुकुन्दब्रह्मेन्द्रप्रभृतिपरिवारं विजयते
तवागारं रम्यं त्रिभुवनमहाराजगृहिणि ॥ 14॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sphurannānāratnasphaṭikamayabhittipratiphala
ttvadākāraṃ chañchachChaśadharakalāsaudhaśikharam ।
mukundabrahmēndraprabhṛtiparivāraṃ vijayatē
tavāgāraṃ ramyaṃ tribhuvanamahārājagṛhiṇi ॥ 14॥
contentBlocks.translation: In a palace whose walls of crystals and gems reflect your glowing form with the moving crescent-moon on its top — [your form] is being worshipped by Viṣṇu (Mukunda), Brahmā, and Indra with joined palms. Thus — that half-form of yours with the crescent-moon — let it shine always in my heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: sphurat-nānā-ratna-sphaṭika-maya-bhitti-pratiphala: reflected in glowing gem-and-crystal walls | tvad-ākāram: your glowing form | cañcat-śaśadhara-kalā-saudha-śikharam: with the moving crescent-moon on the palace-top | mukunda-brahma-indra-praṇata: worshipped by Viṣṇu, Brahmā, and Indra with joined palms | ardha-vapuḥ: half-form | lagnam: shining | hṛdi: in my heart

contentBlocks.original:
निवासः कैलासे विधिशतमखाद्याः स्तुतिकराः
कुटुम्बं त्रैलोक्यं कृतकरपुटः सिद्धिनिकरः ।
महेशः प्राणेशस्तदवनिधराधीशतनये
न ते सौभाग्यस्य क्वचिदपि मनागस्ति तुलना ॥ 15॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nivāsaḥ kailāsē vidhiśatamakhādyāḥ stutikarāḥ
kuṭumbaṃ trailōkyaṃ kṛtakarapuṭaḥ siddhinikaraḥ ।
mahēśaḥ prāṇēśastadavanidharādhīśatanayē
na tē saubhāgyasya kvachidapi manāgasti tulanā ॥ 15॥
contentBlocks.translation: Her dwelling is on Kailāsa. Brahmā, Indra, and others are engaged in singing her praises. The three worlds are her family. The assembly of Siddhas [stands with] joined palms. Maheśa stands in front with [his characteristic offerings]. What does this Bhavānī lack?
contentBlocks.meanings: nivāsaḥ kailāse: dwelling on Kailāsa | vidhi-śata-makhādi: Brahmā, Indra and others | stuti-karāḥ: engaged in praising | kuṭumbam trailok-yam: the three worlds as family | kṛta-kara-puṭaḥ siddhi-nikaraḥ: assembly of Siddhas with joined palms | maheśaḥ prāg: Maheśa in front | bhavānyāḥ: of Bhavānī | kim kiñcit hinaṃ: what lacks

contentBlocks.original:
वृषो वृद्धो यानं विषमशनमाशा निवसनं
श्मशानं क्रीडाभूर्भुजगनिवहो भूषणविधिः
समग्रा सामग्री जगति विदितैव स्मररिपोः
यदेतस्यैश्वर्यं तव जननि सौभाग्यमहिमा ॥ 16॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vṛṣō vṛddhō yānaṃ viṣamaśanamāśā nivasanaṃ
śmaśānaṃ krīḍābhūrbhujaganivahō bhūṣaṇavidhiḥ
samagrā sāmagrī jagati viditaiva smararipōḥ
yadētasyaiśvaryaṃ tava janani saubhāgyamahimā ॥ 16॥
contentBlocks.translation: An old bull is the vehicle. Irregular food. The sky is the garment. Cremation ground is the playground. A mass of serpents is the ornament-arrangement. All this [pertaining to Śiva] exists in the world — yet Bhavānī chose him. What does this say about the power of a beautiful woman?
contentBlocks.meanings: vṛṣaḥ vṛddhaḥ yānam: an old bull as vehicle | viṣama-aśanam: irregular food | āśā nivasanam: the sky is the garment | śmaśānam krīḍā-bhūḥ: cremation ground as playground | bhujaga-nivahaḥ bhūṣaṇa-vidhiḥ: mass of serpents as ornaments | samāgrā sāmagrī: all this complete equipment | jagati: in the world | varāṅganā-vaibhavam: the power/glory of a beautiful woman

contentBlocks.original:
अशेषब्रह्माण्डप्रलयविधिनैसर्गिकमतिः
श्मशानेष्वासीनः कृतभसितलेपः पशुपतिः ।
दधौ कण्ठे हालाहलमखिलभूगोलकृपया
भवत्याः सङ्गत्याः फलमिति च कल्याणि कलये ॥ 17॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aśēṣabrahmāṇḍapralayavidhinaisargikamatiḥ
śmaśānēṣvāsīnaḥ kṛtabhasitalēpaḥ paśupatiḥ ।
dadhau kaṇṭhē hālāhalamakhilabhūgōlakṛpayā
bhavatyāḥ saṅgatyāḥ phalamiti cha kalyāṇi kalayē ॥ 17॥
contentBlocks.translation: Paśupati — whose natural disposition is [the total dissolution of all universes] — sitting in the cremation grounds, smeared with sacred ash — held the hālāhala poison in his throat for the sake of all beings. Such is her husband. O daughter of the mountain — what else is [your glory] beyond this?
contentBlocks.meanings: aśeṣa-brahmāṇḍa-pralaya-vidhi-naisargika-matiḥ: whose natural disposition is the dissolution of all universes | śmaśāneṣu āsīnaḥ: sitting in cremation grounds | kṛta-bhasita-lepaḥ: smeared with sacred ash | paśupatiḥ: Paśupati (Śiva) | kanthhe hālāhalam: in the throat, the hālāhala poison | akhila-hitāya: for the sake of all beings | dādhau: held | śaila-tanaye: O daughter of the mountain

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदीयं सौन्दर्यं निरतिशयमालोक्य परया
भियैवासीद्गङ्गा जलमयतनुः शैलतनये ।
तदेतस्यास्तस्माद्वदनकमलं वीक्ष्य कृपया
प्रतिष्ठामातन्वन्निजशिरसिवासेन गिरिशः ॥ 18॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadīyaṃ saundaryaṃ niratiśayamālōkya parayā
bhiyaivāsīdgaṅgā jalamayatanuḥ śailatanayē ।
tadētasyāstasmādvadanakamalaṃ vīkṣya kṛpayā
pratiṣṭhāmātanvannijaśirasivāsēna giriśaḥ ॥ 18॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having seen your incomparable beauty, O daughter of the mountain — the very Gaṅgā, whose body is made of water, was seized with such fear [of being compared unfavorably] that she became the white foam on the water — and from that time, her foam-drop at the corner of your face is said to be the dot of white on the dark night-sky.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvadīyam saundarya: your beauty | niratiśayam: incomparable | ālokyā: having seen | paraya bhiyā: with great fear | eva: indeed | āsīt: was | gaṅgā: Gaṅgā | jala-maya-tanuḥ: whose body is made of water | śaila-tanaye: O daughter of the mountain | tad-etasya: from that | prasāda: favor/result | vada-na-kamale: at the corner of your face | śveta: white | vṛṣṭiḥ iva: like drops of rain

contentBlocks.original:
विशालश्रीखण्डद्रवमृगमदाकीर्णघुसृण
प्रसूनव्यामिश्रं भगवति तवाभ्यङ्गसलिलम् ।
समादाय स्रष्टा चलितपदपांसून्निजकरैः
समाधत्ते सृष्टिं विबुधपुरपङ्केरुहदृशाम् ॥ 19॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśālaśrīkhaṇḍadravamṛgamadākīrṇaghusṛṇa
prasūnavyāmiśraṃ bhagavati tavābhyaṅgasalilam ।
samādāya sraṣṭā chalitapadapāṃsūnnijakaraiḥ
samādhattē sṛṣṭiṃ vibudhapurapaṅkēruhadṛśām ॥ 19॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Bhagavatī — the wide unguent-water for your bath — mixed with sandalwood liquid, musk, saffron, fragrant substances, and flowers — was taken by Brahmā, who then departed eagerly, having used it to create the many-colored universe.
contentBlocks.meanings: viśāla-śrī-khaṇḍa-drava: wide sandalwood liquid | mṛga-mada-ākīrṇa: mixed with musk | ghuṣṛṇa: saffron | prasūna-vyāmiśram: mixed with flowers | bhagavati: O Bhagavatī | tava abhyaṅga-salilaṃ: your bath-water | samādāya: having taken | sraṣṭā: Brahmā (the creator) | calita-mānasa: with eager mind | nānā-varṇa: many-colored | jagat: universe | sṛṣṭi: having created

contentBlocks.original:
वसन्ते सानन्दे कुसुमितलताभिः परिवृते
स्फुरन्नानापद्मे सरसि कलहंसालिसुभगे ।
सखीभिः खेलन्तीं मलयपवनान्दोलितजले
स्मरेद्यस्त्वां तस्य ज्वरजनितपीडापसरति ॥ 20॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vasantē sānandē kusumitalatābhiḥ parivṛtē
sphurannānāpadmē sarasi kalahaṃsālisubhagē ।
sakhībhiḥ khēlantīṃ malayapavanāndōlitajalē
smarēdyastvāṃ tasya jvarajanitapīḍāpasarati ॥ 20॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the joyful spring — surrounded by flowering creepers — in a lotus-filled lake graced by beautiful geese — O you of beautiful yellow complexion — you were playing with your companions in the Malaya-mountain breeze — [thus the poet meditates on you].
contentBlocks.meanings: vasante sānande: in the joyful spring | kusumita-latābhiḥ parivṛte: surrounded by flowering creepers | sphurat-nānā-padme: with many shining lotuses | sarasi: in the lake | kala-haṃsa-āli-subhage: graced by beautiful geese | sakhībhiḥ khelantīm: playing with companions | malaya-ānila-bhogate: enjoying the Malaya-mountain breeze | pīta-varṇe: O you of beautiful yellow complexion
